name: Sarit Hadad
description: '''Sarit Hadad''' ({{langx|he|שרית חדד}}, {{IPA|he|saˈʁit χaˈdad| }}; born '''Sarah Hudadatov''', 20 September 1978) is an Israeli singer. In October 2009, she was named by the Israeli [[Channel 24 (Israel)|Channel 24]] as the "best female singer of the 2000s". She represented [[Israel]] with the song "[[Nadlik Beyakhad Ner|Light a Candle]]" at the [[Eurovision Song Contest 2002]] in [[Tallin]], [[Estonia]].
